Bus Timings

F62 Bus Schedule Nadd Al Hamar to Umm Ramool and Return

March 31, 2026
By Al Khaleej
Install
★★★★★ 5.0
Google Play Official App
UAE Bus Timing

Travel across the UAE with confidence. No confusion. No wasted time.

Bus F62 provides a vital transportation link between Nadd Al Hamar and Umm Ramool, offering a reliable and affordable travel option for residents and visitors. Running in a loop from Nadd Al Hamar Terminus through several key stops and back, this route ensures convenient access to educational institutions, shopping centers, and commercial areas. Whether commuting to work, attending school, or running errands, Bus F62 is a flexible option for those traveling within Dubai.

Operational Details

With early morning to late-night service, it accommodates different schedules, ensuring that passengers have access to transportation whenever needed. Here are the key timings for the service:

From Nadd Al Hamar Terminus From Umm Ramool, Street 25
05:00 06:04
05:08 07:04
06:00 07:34
06:08 08:09

Bus F62 Timetable

For full details on the F62 bus schedule, visit the official RTA website or click here for F62 Bus Timings.

Ticket Pricing

Bus F62 offers an affordable fare of AED 5 for travel between Nadd Al Hamar and Umm Ramool, making it an economical option for both daily commuters and occasional travelers. The fare structure for Dubai’s public transport system is based on the number of zones traveled, and since the F62 route typically covers one zone, the fare remains consistent at AED 5.

Route Overview

Bus F62 starts its journey at the Nadd Al Hamar Terminus and takes passengers through several key locations before reaching Umm Ramool, Street 25, and looping back to Nadd Al Hamar. The route offers stops at malls, schools, business centers, and residential areas, providing a balanced service that caters to both commuters and casual travelers. Key stops along the route include:

  1. Nadd Al Hamar Terminus The starting and ending point of the journey, connecting passengers to various local and regional transport options.

  2. Al Telal 17 A residential stop catering to the local community.

  3. Al Awal Building A convenient stop for residents and visitors in the area.

  4. Al Zarooni Building Providing easy access to residential and commercial locations.

  5. Al Zarooni Masjid A community stop offering access to a local mosque for worshippers.

  6. The City School International A key stop for students and staff at the school.

  7. Avenues A shopping and entertainment destination along the route.

  8. One Square Mall A popular stop for shoppers in the area.

  9. Aswaq Mall A convenient retail hub for nearby residents.

  10. Health Center Providing easy access to healthcare services.

  11. Nadd Al Hamar Park A recreational stop for families and visitors.

  12. Al Badia Entrance Gateway to the Al Badia residential community.

  13. Deira International School A key stop for students, parents, and school staff.

  14. Al Badia Residences Serving residents of this popular residential area.

  15. Hillside Village A A community-focused stop for residents in this neighborhood.

  16. Dubai Festival City, Festival Centre A major shopping and entertainment destination along the route.

Recommended Guides

Enhance your daily commute with these essential transport guides:

Complete RTA Intercity Routes Guide Largest Bus Stations Infrastructure in UAE

  1. Dubai Festival City, Marks & Spencer A well-known landmark for shopping enthusiasts.

  2. Sheikh Zayed Housing Program A stop providing access to residential and community services.

  3. Al Garhoud, Cambridge International School A key stop for the school community.

  4. American College of Dubai A stop serving students and staff at the college.

  5. Grammar School Another important stop for the educational community.

  6. Roads & Transport Authority HQ A central hub for Dubai’s transport operations.

  7. Mawakeb School A stop for students and staff at this educational institution.

  8. Al Futtaim Auto Center A key stop for those accessing automotive services.

  9. Dubai Commer City A business hub with several stops for ease of access.

  10. Emirates Metro Providing seamless connectivity to the Dubai Metro system.

  11. Umm Ramool, Street 25 The midpoint of the journey and a significant hub in the Umm Ramool area.

  12. Street 25, Khansaheb Serving nearby industrial and residential areas.

  13. Nadd Al Hamar Road, Service Road A key stop for commuters in this busy area.

  14. Areen East West A stop providing access to residential and commercial facilities.

  15. Holiday Inn A stop for hotel guests and travelers.

Nearby Attractions

Bus F62 provides convenient access to several key landmarks and attractions, making it a great choice for both commuters and visitors. Key attractions along the route include:

  • Dubai Festival City

  • Deira International School
  • Al Badia Park

Journey Experience

Bus F62 offers a comfortable and efficient journey from Nadd Al Hamar to Umm Ramool and back, passing through key areas like One Square Mall and Dubai Festival City. With air-conditioned seating and accessible features for people of determination (PoD), the bus ensures a smooth ride for all passengers.

Whether you’re heading to work or exploring the city, Bus F62 provides a reliable, hassle-free travel experience.

Bus F62 serves as a convenient and efficient transport option for those traveling between Nadd Al Hamar and Umm Ramool. With well-placed stops, frequent service, and a direct route, it caters to both daily commuters and occasional travelers. Whether you’re heading to work, shopping, or exploring the city, Bus F62 offers an affordable and reliable way to navigate Dubai. For more detailed schedule and route information, be sure to consult the official RTA resources.

Official UAE Sources

For the latest schedules, fares, and policy updates, verify with these primary sources:

  • RTA Journey Planner
  • RTA Intercity Timetable Reference (E100)
  • Abu Dhabi Mobility
  • Sharjah Roads and Transport Authority (SRTA)

Editorial review date: March 31, 2026.

Written by Al Khaleej
